title: +
content:
+
+1. **Bidirectional Flow** – Modules communicate upward, downward and laterally; high‑level context constrains low‑level interpretation and vice versa【3†L47-L67】.
+2. **Unified Representation** – A central hypergraph stores multimodal meaning representations; all modules read from and write to this structure【33635101037775†L253-L272】.
+3. **Multimodal Fusion** – Text, audio, images, gestures and icons map into a shared semantic space via cross‑modal attention and transformers【33635101037775†L184-L221】.
+4. **Ethical Gating** – Dual modes (RAW and PUBLIC) enforce safety and cultural sensitivity. Raw mode is unconstrained; public mode routes outputs through fact, bias and culture gates【3†L123-L145】.
+5. **Meta‑learning Controller** – A supervisor monitors performance, manages resources and resolves conflicting interpretations. It directs meta‑learning (self‑improvement) and enforces drift budgets.
+
+### Logical Invariants
+
+- **Faithfulness** – Semantic similarity to source must stay above a predefined threshold (e.g., 0.85). All transformations must preserve truthfulness and core meaning.
